memes.label: "Abdullah"
memes.description: "Saïd's friend turned betrayer in Pickthall's novel, a fisherman who exploits trust for material gain."

memes.label: "Alms"
memes.description: "Charitable giving to the poor, a fundamental religious obligation in Islam; in the novel, almsgiving marks the social contract between rich and poor and the moral character of the giver."

memes.label: "Authority"
memes.description: "The concept of legitimate power and command in Saïd the Fisherman, exercised by officials, soldiers, and slave-drivers who control access to justice and movement through Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Bazaar"
memes.description: "The bazaar (sūq) as the commercial and social heart of Ottoman Damascus in Saïd the Fisherman, a space of trade, rumor, and social encounter."

memes.label: "Beggar"
memes.description: "One who lives by asking alms; in the novel, the condition to which Saïd is reduced, and the disguise adopted by the mysterious old man who guides his redemption."

memes.label: "Boat"
memes.description: "A small watercraft used for transport and trade on the Nile and Mediterranean; in the novel, boats mark transitions between lands, identities, and phases of Saïd's journey."

memes.label: "Coffee"
memes.description: "The cultural and social role of coffee and coffeehouses in Saïd the Fisherman, where the coffeehouse functions as a central institution for male sociability, business, news-gathering, and the ritual of hospitality in Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Court"
memes.description: "The Ottoman judicial court (mehkemeh) in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — an institution of law, corruption, and social control in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Curse"
memes.description: "The speech act of cursing in Saïd the Fisherman, functioning as verbal aggression, supernatural invocation, and social commentary in moments of high emotional intensity."

memes.label: "Damascus"
memes.description: "The primary setting of Saïd the Fisherman, the great city of late Ottoman Syria where commerce, religion, and intrigue intersect."

memes.label: "Debt"
memes.description: "A sum of money owed; in the novel, the unpaid debts that contributed to Saïd's financial ruin and the broader theme of obligation — to God, to creditors, to one's own past."

memes.label: "Deceit"
memes.description: "The act of intentional deception in Saïd the Fisherman, where deceit operates as a social weapon used to undermine reputation and standing in the competitive world of Damascus."

memes.label: "Dragoman"
memes.description: "The dragoman as a cultural and linguistic intermediary in Saïd the Fisherman, serving as translator, guide, and broker between Ottoman subjects and European travelers in the late Ottoman Levant."

memes.label: "Effendi"
memes.description: "The honorific title Effendi in Saïd the Fisherman, marking social rank, education, and respectability in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Faith"
memes.description: "Religious faith and interpersonal faithfulness in the late Ottoman world of Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing Islamic devotion, sectarian identity, and the moral virtue of loyalty."

memes.label: "Feast"

memes.label: "Ferideh"

memes.label: "Fisherman"
memes.description: "The profession and social identity of Saïd, the protagonist, representing the intersection of livelihood, social status, and maritime culture in the late Ottoman Levant."

memes.label: "Garden"
memes.description: "The pleasure-groves and gardens of Damascus, spaces of leisure, conspiracy, and natural beauty"

memes.label: "Gift"
memes.description: "Something given voluntarily without expectation of return; in the novel, gifts create bonds of obligation, express gratitude, and sometimes conceal darker purposes."

memes.label: "Gratitude"
memes.description: "The moral obligation of thankfulness in Saïd the Fisherman, where gratitude binds beneficiaries to benefactors and ingratitude is among the gravest of social sins."

memes.label: "Guest"
memes.description: "The sacred obligation of hospitality toward guests in Saïd the Fisherman, where the guest-host relationship is governed by ritual courtesy, spatial separation, and religious duty."

memes.label: "Hasneh"
memes.description: "A key female character in Saïd the Fisherman, whose relationships and actions shape the novel's exploration of love, jealousy, and the position of women in Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Honor"
memes.description: "The concept of honor as social standing and moral reputation in Saïd the Fisherman, where wealth, lineage, and public esteem determine a person's place in the honor-based society of Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Hospitality"
memes.description: "The sacred duty of hospitality in Saïd the Fisherman, where the obligation to welcome and protect guests is a fundamental social and religious value in Ottoman Damascene culture, and its breach is considered the blackest of crimes."

memes.label: "Host"
memes.description: "One who receives and entertains guests; in the novel, the host embodies the virtues and perils of hospitality — generosity, protection, and the potential for betrayal at the table."

memes.label: "Household"

memes.label: "Infidel"
memes.description: "The concept of infidel (kâfir) in Saïd the Fisherman, designating religious outsiders and functioning as a boundary marker of Muslim communal identity in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Ismaìl Abbâs"
memes.description: "A wealthy host renowned for hospitality, wisdom, and learning, whose gatherings represent elite Damascene culture"

memes.label: "Journey"
memes.description: "Saïd's overland and maritime journey from Damascus to the Levantine coast in Pickthall's novel — a physical passage that mirrors spiritual transformation and marks the narrative's pivot from prosperity to destitution and back."

memes.label: "Judge"
memes.description: "A public official appointed to decide cases in a court of law; in the novel, the figure who embodies both the authority of the state and the ideal of impartial justice."

memes.label: "Justice"
memes.description: "The concept of justice in Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing both the formal Ottoman legal system represented by courts and officials, and the popular impulse toward vigilante justice when institutions fail to deliver fair outcomes."

memes.label: "Khan"
memes.description: "A roadside inn or caravanserai providing lodging for travellers and merchants; in the novel, a recurring setting for arrivals, transactions, and moments of crisis."

memes.label: "Lawyer"
memes.description: "The role of the legal advocate in Saïd the Fisherman — a figure who mediates between petitioners and the Qadi's court, wielding rhetorical skill and procedural knowledge as instruments of access to justice in Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Livelihood"
memes.description: "The concept of livelihood as the means of material survival in Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing fishing, trade, and the precarious economic conditions that drive the protagonist's journey from poverty to prosperity and back."

memes.label: "Mahmud"
memes.description: "Obstinate merchant who holds Saïd's wealth in trust, his stubbornness a source of narrative tension"

memes.label: "Master"
memes.description: "One who has authority, ownership, or control over servants, property, or circumstances; in the novel, an identity Saïd claims, loses, and must relearn."

memes.label: "Merchant"
memes.description: "A dealer in goods and commodities; in the novel, the social identity Saïd adopts after attaining wealth, central to his rise and fall in Damashc-ush-Shâm."

memes.label: "Oath"
memes.description: "The oath as a binding verbal contract and religious invocation in Saïd the Fisherman, carrying legal weight before the Qadi and social weight in everyday speech."

memes.label: "Prayer"
memes.description: "Islamic prayer practice as depicted in Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing the ritual five daily prayers, personal supplication, and the social and moral significance of devotion in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Prophet"
memes.description: "The figure of the Prophet Muhammad and the concept of prophethood in Saïd the Fisherman, representing divine authority, intercession, and the religious identity of the Muslim community."

memes.label: "Quran"
memes.description: "The Qurʾān in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — the sacred text of Islam, invoked in oaths, quoted in argument, and ever-present as a material object of beauty and authority in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Riot"
memes.description: "The sectarian uprising in Damascus, where the faithful rage against Christians and social order collapses"

memes.label: "River"
memes.description: "The great waterway of Egypt, central to the geography of the novel; the Nile at Rashìd is the setting for key scenes of arrival, departure, and transit."

memes.label: "Saïd"
memes.description: "The protagonist of Marmaduke Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman, a fisherman of Damascus who rises to wealth and falls back to poverty through cycles of fortune and betrayal."

memes.label: "Sea"
memes.description: "The Mediterranean Sea and Levantine coast in Marmaduke Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — a space of livelihood, danger, prayer, and transformation that mirrors the protagonist's inner journey."

memes.label: "Selìm"
memes.description: "A loyal friend and protector of Saïd in Pickthall's novel, representing steadfast friendship and compassion in a narrative world of betrayal."

memes.label: "Servant"
memes.description: "A person employed to perform domestic or personal services for a master; in the novel, servants mediate between social classes and witness the intimate lives of their masters."

memes.label: "Shame"
memes.description: "The social emotion of shame in Saïd the Fisherman, functioning as a mechanism of social control and a personal experience of dishonor in the honor-based culture of late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Sin"
memes.description: "The concept of sin as moral transgression in Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing violations of divine law, social betrayal, and the narrative's moral framework for judging character actions."

memes.label: "Song"
memes.description: "Song and oral poetry in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — the rhythms of work, love, and lamentation that give voice to characters' inner lives and mark the novel's emotional register."

memes.label: "Storm"
memes.description: "The maritime storm in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — a physical and spiritual crisis at sea during which the protagonist confronts mortality, divine power, and the limits of human agency."

memes.label: "Tavern"
memes.description: "The coffeehouse-tavern of Abu Khalìl and Rashìd in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — a space of male sociability, storytelling, and subterranean commerce at the margins of Damascus society."

memes.label: "Trade"
memes.description: "Trade as an economic activity and social force in Saïd the Fisherman, the primary means by which the protagonist attempts to rise above his station as a fisherman in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Truth"
memes.description: "The concept of truth as both factual accuracy and moral honesty in the narrative world of Saïd the Fisherman, where truth-telling is a marker of character and falsehood drives the plot's reversals."

memes.label: "Usury"
memes.description: "The practice of lending money at interest, forbidden in Islamic law; in the novel, a practice associated with foreign merchants and moral corruption."

memes.label: "Voyage"
memes.description: "Saïd's sea voyage on the steamer, a journey of displacement, encounter, and transformation"

memes.label: "Wâly"
memes.description: "The Wâly (Ottoman provincial governor) in Pickthall's Saïd the Fisherman — the executive authority in Damascus whose power over property, police, and patronage shapes the novel's political landscape."

memes.label: "Wealth"
memes.description: "A central thematic concept in Saïd the Fisherman, representing material prosperity and its effects on social standing, personal relationships, and moral character in late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Wisdom"
memes.description: "The cultural value of wisdom in Saïd the Fisherman, encompassing religious knowledge, practical shrewdness, and the learned discourse prized by the elite of late Ottoman Damascus."

memes.label: "Witness"
memes.description: "One who gives evidence or testimony; in the novel, the act of calling God as witness to the truth of one's words, a ubiquitous speech act that sacralizes assertion and binds speaker to truth."

memes.label: "Yuhanna"
memes.description: "A Nazarene (Christian) merchant whose wealth becomes entangled with Saïd's fortunes"
